Title: Molecular basis of the key regulator WRINKLED1 in plant oil biosynthesis

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Columbia ecotype; wri1-1 background in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/EREBP family; tandem AP2 DNA-binding domains; C-terminal intrinsically disordered region with transactivation/PEST motif mentioned
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seeds; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major component of human diet and industrial feedstock
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via binding the AW-box cis-element in promoters of oil-biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AW-box-containing target genes; BCCP2 promoter AW-box used as cognate binding sequence
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds cognate AW-box with high affinity and activates oil-biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: pivotal regulator/key regulator governing plant oil accumulation; DNA-binding strength correlates with oil production
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plants grown at 23°C under 16 h light/8 h dark for Arabidopsis and N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AW-box dsDNA in BCCP2 promoter; 14-3-3 proteins; KIN10
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: binds DNA as a 1:1 AtWRI1-dsDNA complex; protein complex with 14-3-3 and KIN10 discussed from prior studies
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive, transient constructs); proAtWRI1/native Arabidopsis WRI1 promoter (native promoter, stable Arabidopsis lines)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: x-ray crystallography of AtWRI1-DNA complex; ITC DNA-binding assay; site-directed mutagenesis; Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression with TAG quantification; stable complementation of Arabidopsis wri1-1 with seed FA analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes oil/TAG and fatty acid accumulation; engineered W74R variant further enhances oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for bioengineering to improve vegetable oil yield; W74R substitution is a rational engineering strategy applicable across WRI1 orthologs to increase oil production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtWRI1 binds the AW-box dsDNA with high affinity (Kd ~0.48 μM) using tandem AP2 domains in a previously unrecognized DNA-binding mode. 2. DNA-contact residues H72, E79, Q98, H175, E181, and especially R183 are critical for binding; reduced-binding variants show reduced TAG accumulation in Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. Structure-guided engineering of AtWRI1 W74R increased DNA-binding affinity about 10-fold, increased TAG in transient Nicotiana leaves by ~60%, and increased seed fatty acid content in stable transgenic Arabidopsis wri1-1 lines.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core subject of experimentation is AtWRI1 and its engineered variants; orthologs from Brassica napus, Camelina sativa, Glycine max, and Zea mays were tested only to generalize the W-to-R engineering concept. The study focuses on DNA-binding mechanism rather than comprehensive target-gene catalog.
